author: mark
text:
Neutralize terminal control sequences in rendered task content

bl show/list/ready and the dep-tree/prime renderers wrote task title, description, tags, notes, external slices, branch and repo raw to the TTY. The only escape-aware code was column-width math that deliberately passed ANSI through, so the render pipeline assumed its content was trusted.

That holds only while every task is locally authored. Bidirectional external sync makes those fields attacker-controlled: a hostile string can forge bl list/show output (hide or spoof a task, fake an id/owner), poison the clipboard via OSC 52 so a later paste runs an attacker command, or spoof a hyperlink via OSC 8. External content is data, not terminal commands.

New src/sanitize.rs is the single display-boundary chokepoint. inline() neutralizes all C0/C1/DEL plus tab and newline for single-line cells; block() keeps only newline and tab for the wrapped description. Replacement is strictly 1:1 (one control char -> one U+FFFD) so the list-truncation width math stays correct without an ANSI parser. Callers sanitize content before composing balls' own SGR styling; --json output never passes through here, keeping the machine contract byte-exact.

Ingest-side bounds (max title length, tag count/charset, max creates-per-sync, unbounded verbatim external map) remain out of scope and unfiled. 100% coverage; clippy clean.
